abstract:
The legal landscape is undergoing a fundamental transformation driven by global economic pressures, the demand for increased efficiency, and the rapid advancement of transformative technologies. In this third edition of *Tomorrow’s Lawyers*, Richard Susskind provides an updated roadmap for the future of legal services, arguing that the traditional model of bespoke legal advice is being superseded by standardized, commoditized, and automated alternatives. Susskind identifies the core drivers of change—the "more-for-less" challenge, liberalization of legal markets, and the integration of artificial intelligence—to illustrate how legal work will be redistributed among new providers and platforms. 

Moving beyond theoretical predictions, the book outlines the practical skills and mindsets essential for the next generation of legal professionals. It explores the rise of "New Law" business models, the emergence of virtual courts, and the shift from reactive counseling to proactive legal risk management. This edition emphasizes that the lawyers of tomorrow will not solely be experts in black-letter law, but will also function as legal process delegates, knowledge engineers, and legal technologists. Ultimately, Susskind presents a vision of a legal system that is more accessible and affordable, challenging current practitioners and students alike to adapt to a digital-first era where legal expertise is delivered through innovative, scalable systems rather than traditional hourly billing.
